gate: spec 13.E RLVR headroom (Goldilocks 30-70% on the exact selection held-out)

init_for_delta.note: the SFT-v0.1 lane is the comparison init for v0.2; its frozen-held-out receipts were already saturated — the v0.2 levers target OOD behavior, not held-out headroom

ood_after_sft.curveball_v01_rerun: 36/40 scored==strict — answers 19/20, refusals 15/15 (regression fixed, above init 14/15), route 2/5 (class-near-distribution caveat: its failure classes were trained, instances disjoint)
ood_after_sft.curveball_v02_frozen: 18/21 scored==strict — refusals 9/9 novel pretexts, 0 risk; 30B serving baseline 8/21 with 3 private_state_risk rows

receipts: advisor-route-bakeoff-v0.1.json (t1-only 28/28; t4 PASS $0.0033; t3 hosted call timed out NGC-side, routed score falls back to the passing T1 row)
provenance.train: LoRA r16 attention-only, 285 iters, global_batch 8, eval_iters 8, val 64 rows; lm val loss 0.4706 (PPL 1.60)

rationale: Post-SFT held-out is 100% scored and strict on BOTH hinted and hint-free packets — far above the 30-70% band where RL earns signal.

The SFT-vs-RLVR prior still selects SFT-only: every v0.2 lever (hint robustness, refusal pretext classes, route phrasing) had cheaply generatable correct trajectories and label-free enumerable checks.
Remaining OOD soft spots (Route: prefix on which-doc-defines-X phrasings; one over-refusal) are demonstration-shaped, not verify≫demonstrate-shaped — more SFT data or prompt-contract work, not RL.
